top_picks_subtitle: Hand-tested and reviewed by our team of experts
trust_section_content: Home Nura has established itself as the leading authority in smart kitchen appliance reviews since 2020. Our testing methodology relies on rigorous, transparent criteria: energy performance, cooking speed, actual capacity, heating uniformity, and durability. Each air fryer undergoes approximately 40 hours of laboratory testing, subjected to over 100 complete cooking cycles with various foods. We rank results using the Nura Score, our proprietary rating system that weights: 25% thermal performance, 20% energy efficiency, 20% ease of use, 18% build quality, and 17% value for money. Our tests are completely independent - no manufacturer funds our reviews. For 6 years, we've partnered with ISO 17025 certified laboratories to guarantee absolute objectivity and consistency across all European markets we cover.
market_section_title: The UK Air Fryer Market in 2026
market_section_content: The United Kingdom is experiencing explosive growth in the air fryer market. In 2026, over 41% of British households own at least one air fryer, up from just 16% in 2022. The sector generates over £650 million annually. Leading brands include Philips (28% market share), Ninja (24%), Cosori (16%), Tefal (12%), Sage (11%), and others (9%). Prices range from £39 for entry-level models to over £600 for premium smart variants. However, the majority of British consumers concentrate purchases in the £120-300 range where quality is consistently strong. Current trends show robust demand for WiFi-connected models (45% of sales), XXL capacity units for larger households (38% annual growth), and dual-basket designs (31% uptake). The British preference for convenient, energy-efficient cooking solutions has made air fryers mainstream across all demographic groups, from young professionals to multi-generational family homes.
testing_section_content: Our testing protocol covers five critical dimensions. First, thermal performance: we measure actual temperature across multiple chamber points and compare against displayed settings using calibrated infrared sensors. Second, cooking consistency: each model prepares identical portions of standard test foods (potato wedges, chicken cutlets, broccoli) with visual assessment and colour analysis for browning uniformity. Third, actual energy consumption: we measure real-world power draw during 50 complete cooking cycles at a wall socket. Fourth, usability: control ergonomics, display readability, smartphone app functionality where applicable, and ease of cleaning. Fifth, longevity: mechanical reliability testing, gasket integrity after 500 cycles, thermal stability over extended use periods. The Nura Score synthesises all data into a 0-100 rating enabling objective comparison of every model we test.
recommendation_section_title: Quick Guide: Which Air Fryer for You?
recommendation_section_content: Starting out and want budget-friendly? The Cosori Lite (2.6L) and Tefal Compact (4L) deliver excellent value under £120, with solid performance and intuitive controls perfect for first-time buyers. Large household (5+ people)? Invest in XXL models like the Ninja AF500 (5.2L) or Philips 6L XL that handle family meals efficiently, with dual-basket options if you need simultaneous cooking. Tech-savvy and want app control? The Ninja Foodi Smart (WiFi-enabled) and Philips Connected range offer full smartphone control, intelligent presets, and cooking history tracking. Energy conscious and want to cut bills? Cosori Pro (1500W) and Tefal Precision (1400W) consume 15-20% less power than average models while maintaining top-tier thermal performance. Noise sensitive? Philips Compact (under 70dB) and Ninja Whisper Series operate quietly, ideal for open-plan kitchens. On a tight budget under £80? Cecotec Compact (2L) and Costway Ultra (1.8L) deliver no-compromise frying performance without premium pricing. Active cooks wanting menu variety? Philips Premium Series includes 200+ recipes; Ninja Foodi offers 101 preset programmes covering everything from appetisers to desserts.
guide_intro_title: How to choose the best air fryer in 2026?
guide_intro_text: The UK air fryer market continues to grow rapidly, with models from Ninja, Philips, Cosori and Tefal dominating. Our comprehensive comparison guide helps you find the ideal air fryer based on your budget, family size and cooking habits.
guide_criteria_capacity: Capacity: 2-3L for 1-2 people, 4-5L for 3-4 people, 6L+ for large families. Dual basket models let you cook two dishes simultaneously.
guide_criteria_power: Power: between 1000W and 2200W. Higher wattage means faster preheating. Models with 1400-1800W offer the best balance.
guide_criteria_features: Features: automatic programmes, WiFi connectivity, timer, keep warm. Connected models allow smartphone control.

faq.question: How does an air fryer work?
faq.answer: An air fryer uses a powerful fan to circulate very hot air around food, creating a crispy finish similar to deep frying but with up to 80% less fat. Rapid Air technology ensures even heat distribution for consistent results every time.

faq.question: What size air fryer do I need for my family?
faq.answer: For 1-2 people, a 2-3L model is sufficient. For a family of 3-4, go for 4-5L. For 5+ people, choose an XXL model of 6L or more. Dual basket models let you cook two dishes simultaneously, perfect for busy households.

faq.question: Do air fryers use a lot of electricity?
faq.answer: No, air fryers are very energy efficient. They typically use 1000-1700W and cook faster than a conventional oven, reducing energy consumption by 30-50%. Over a year, you could save £40-60 on your electricity bill compared to using a traditional oven.

faq.question: Can you cook frozen food in an air fryer?
faq.answer: Absolutely! Air fryers are brilliant with frozen foods: chips, nuggets, onion rings, breaded vegetables. No need to defrost first. Cooking time is typically 20% shorter than an oven and the results are crispier.

faq.question: What is the difference between an air fryer and a convection oven?
faq.answer: An air fryer is essentially a compact, concentrated convection oven. Its smaller size allows for more intense air circulation and near-instant preheating. The result: faster cooking, crispier food, and reduced energy consumption compared to a full-size oven.
